title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. diversifolia
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. diversifolia, also known as False Lavender or Cotton Lavender, is a perennial shrubby plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. diversifolia include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
